data.content_ref: inline:The mechanism relies on a solar eclipse of 1223 BC as a trigger for ideological delegitimation, but the scope explicitly excludes Ugarit and the Levantine city-states while the problem asks why those very polities collapsed. An eclipse visible across the Eastern Mediterranean would have been witnessed in Ugarit and the Levant as well; if eclipse-induced ritual failure explains Mycenaean and Hittite collapse, the account owes an explanation for why Ugarit and the Levantine city-states—which also had palace-temple complexes with cosmic-ritual kingship ideologies—collapsed simultaneously, yet the scope simply excludes them without reconciling this tension. The target thus fails to address the problem's core comparative question by arbitrarily restricting scope to cases its mechanism fits while omitting contemporaneous collapses the mechanism should also implicate.
